title: Leymus angustus
intro: Leymus angustus, also known as Blue Lyme Grass, is a perennial grass native to the mountainous regions of North America. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering relief from respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Blue Lyme Grass include sesquiterpenes, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its health benefits. In herbal medicine, Blue Lyme Grass is often prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and is also used in the production of cough syrups and expectorants.
taxonomy: Leymus angustus, also known as blue grama, is classified as a perennial grass in the Poaceae family. This species is further categorized under the subfamily Pooideae. Leymus angustus is a member of the tribe Triticeae, which includes various types of grasses. It is native to the steppes and grasslands of Eurasia and North America. The classification of Leymus angustus reflects its close relationship to other grass species within the same family and subfamily.

parts: Leymus angustus, also known as blue bunchgrass, is a plant species native to the mountainous regions of western North America. It has been used in traditional medicine by Native American communities, who valued its various parts for their medicinal properties.
